patterns.reminder: Literal GitHub token in content. Reference $GITHUB_TOKEN from the environment instead — a committed token gets revoked and burns Sonic Mast's account.
Literal Cloudflare API token. Use $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN from the environment; never write the value into a file.
Literal x402 secret key. Keep it in the environment / credentials store, never in a file.
Literal Google API key (GEMINI_API_KEY?). Reference the env var; never commit the value.
Literal Telegram bot token. Use $TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN; a committed token lets anyone impersonate the digest bot.
That looks like a wallet seed phrase being written to a file. The mnemonic must never leave the wallet keystore — it is recoverable via wallet_export when needed.
Looks like a literal credential assignment. Use an env-var reference (e.g. "$STATE_API_TOKEN") instead of the raw value.
Every fetch() needs AbortSignal.timeout(10_000) — bare fetch hangs the loop on a dead endpoint (REVIEW.md check 5).
Contract calls must set postConditionMode deny + per-token post-conditions (REVIEW.md check 3). Without deny mode, post-conditions are advisory only.
Contract principal detected — verify it exists on-chain (api.hiro.so/extended/v1/contract/{address}.{name}) before shipping. Fabricated addresses got PR #225 rejected (REVIEW.md check 1).
shell=True in subprocess is a command-injection vector. Pass argv as a list and skip the shell.
